blocks.content: He stood up slowly and walked to the front and looked at the room — all the people, all the faces, everyone who had loved her — and he said:

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I was sitting on a park bench, drawing, when I first saw her. She was walking past and she turned around and looked at me for a second. Just a second. I don't know what I thought in that moment. I don't think I thought anything. I just knew I was going to remember that face.
His voice was steady. He didn't know how.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: She told me about her condition on a November night. I walked home afterward and I thought about what it meant — the kind of love that has a specific weight to it, a specific kind of courage required. And I thought: I want to be the person who is capable of that. I want to be worthy of it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I don't know if I always was. I tried. Every single day I tried to see her the way she deserved to be seen. To hold everything she gave me without dropping any of it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I have two hundred and forty sketchbooks. Her name is in every one of them. From the first page of the first one to the last thing I drew before—

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: She is the best thing that ever happened to me. She is the reason I know what I am capable of. And I am going to love her for the rest of my life.
